title: World Language Typology and NLP Divergences
description: A diagram illustrating major language typologies and the resulting structural divergences that present challenges for NLP systems.
relationships: Divergence in canonical word order creates challenges for parsing and machine translation
Head directionality affects phrase structure and dependency parsing
Morphological complexity (fusion/agglutination) impacts tokenization and morphological analysis
These typological differences necessitate language-specific modeling and robust cross-lingual transfer techniques in NLP
